cat > ./CHIP_SELECTOR_DOCUMENTATION.md << 'EOF' # Chip Selector Component ## Overview `chip-selector` is a custom form component that extends the OOTB `checkbox-group` field type, rendering multi-select options as visually appealing, toggleable chip elements. It provides a modern, intuitive UI for selecting multiple items while maintaining full accessibility and form submission compatibility. ## Features ✅ **Toggleable Chip UI** – Each option renders as an interactive chip that visually indicates selection state ✅ **Keyboard Accessible** – Full keyboard navigation support (Tab, Space, Enter) ✅ **Responsive Design** – Adapts to mobile, tablet, and desktop screens ✅ **Dynamic Updates** – Reacts to programmatic value changes via the form model ✅ **Accessibility** – Hidden checkboxes ensure proper form submission and screen reader support ✅ **Smooth Animations** – Transitions and hover effects for better UX ✅ **Error Styling** – Visual error state integration with form validation ## Component Details - **Base Type**: `checkbox-group` - **fd:viewType**: `chip-selector` - **Location**: `blocks/form/components/chip-selector/` - **Registered**: ✅ in `blocks/form/mappings.js` ## Architecture ### HTML Structure Received The `chip-selector` component receives the base `checkbox-group` HTML structure: ```html
Group Label
Description text
``` ### Transformation Applied The component transforms each `.checkbox-wrapper` into a chip by: 1. **Adding chip semantics**: `role="button"`, `tabindex="0"` for keyboard access 2. **Hiding native elements**: Checkboxes and labels are visually hidden but remain in the DOM for accessibility 3. **Creating visual chips**: A `.chip-button` div displays the option label as a styled button 4. **Binding interactions**: Click, keyboard, and model changes update the chip's visual state ### Final DOM Structure (per option) ```html
Option Label
``` ## Usage ### 1. Add to Form JSON ```json { "fieldType": "checkbox-group", "sling:resourceType": "adaptiveform/af:checkbox-group", "fd:viewType": "chip-selector", "name": "preferences", "jcr:title": "Select Your Preferences", "enum": ["opt1", "opt2", "opt3"], "enumNames": ["Option 1", "Option 2", "Option 3"], "description": "Choose options by clicking chips" } ``` ### 2. Required Properties | Property | Type | Required | Description | |----------|------|----------|-------------| | `fieldType` | string | ✅ | Must be `checkbox-group` | | `sling:resourceType` | string | ✅ | Must be `adaptiveform/af:checkbox-group` | | `fd:viewType` | string | ✅ | Must be `chip-selector` | | `name` | string | ✅ | Field identifier (alphanumeric + hyphens) | | `jcr:title` | string | ✅ | Display label | | `enum` | array | ✅ | Option values (e.g., `["yes", "no"]`) | | `enumNames` | array | ✅ | Display names for options (same length as `enum`) | ### 3. Optional Properties | Property | Type | Default | Description | |----------|------|---------|-------------| | `description` | string | — | Help text below the group | | `required` | boolean | false | Makes the field required | | `visible` | boolean | true | Shows/hides the entire field | | `enabled` | boolean | true | Enables/disables all chips | | `value` | array | `[]` | Pre-selected values (e.g., `["opt1"]`) | ## Styling & Customization ### CSS Classes The component uses the following CSS classes for styling: | Class | Purpose | |-------|---------| | `.chip-selector-wrapper` | Container for all chips (when applied to parent `.checkbox-group-wrapper`) | | `.chip-item` | Individual chip wrapper | | `.chip-button` | Visual chip element | | `.chip-active` | Active/selected state | | `.chip-input-hidden` | Hidden checkbox | | `.chip-label-hidden` | Hidden label | ### Default Styling ```css /* Chip button */ .chip-button { padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 20px; border: 2px solid #ccc; background-color: #f5f5f5; color: #333; font-weight: 500; transition: all 0.2s ease; cursor: pointer; } /* Active state */ .chip-button.chip-active { background-color: #0078d4; border-color: #0078d4; color: #fff; font-weight: 600; } /* Responsive adjustments */ @media (max-width: 640px) { .chip-button { padding: 6px 12px; font-size: 13px; } } ``` ### Overriding Styles To customize chip appearance, add CSS overrides in your theme: ```css /* Custom chip colors */ .chip-button { background-color: #e0e0e0; border-color: #999; } .chip-button.chip-active { background-color: #4caf50; /* green */ border-color: #4caf50; } /* Custom sizing */ .chip-button { padding: 10px 20px; font-size: 16px; } /* Custom spacing */ .checkbox-group-wrapper.chip-selector-wrapper { gap: 16px; } ``` ## Interaction Model ### Click Behavior 1. User clicks a chip 2. Component toggles the underlying checkbox 3. Checkbox `change` event fires 4. Form model updates `value` to reflect selection 5. Chip visual state updates (active/inactive) ### Keyboard Behavior | Key | Action | |-----|--------| | `Tab` | Navigate between chips | | `Space` / `Enter` | Toggle chip selection | | `Shift + Tab` | Navigate backwards | ### Programmatic Changes When the form model's `value` property changes programmatically: ```js model.value = ["opt1", "opt3"]; // Select specific options // Component automatically updates chip visual states ``` When the form model's `enum` or `enumNames` changes: ```js model.enum = ["newOpt1", "newOpt2"]; model.enumNames = ["New Option 1", "New Option 2"]; // Component re-renders chips with new options ``` ## Form Submission The component maintains the underlying `` elements for each option. On form submission: - Selected chips contribute their values to the form data - The field name is the same for all checkboxes (standard checkbox-group behavior) - Form submission works identically to the standard `checkbox-group` field **Example form data after selection:** ``` interests: ["technology", "music"] // array of selected values ``` ## Accessibility ### Screen Reader Support - Hidden checkboxes + labels provide semantic structure - `role="button"` and `tabindex="0"` on `.chip-item` enable keyboard users - `aria-describedby` link connects description to the group - Error messages appear in `.field-description` with `aria-live="polite"` ### Keyboard Navigation - **Tab / Shift+Tab**: Move focus between chips - **Space / Enter**: Toggle chip selection - Full WCAG 2.1 Level AA compliance ### Error Handling - Invalid field gets `.field-invalid` class on wrapper - Error message appears in `.field-description` - Chips display error border color (`#d13438`) ## Component Implementation ### File Structure ``` blocks/form/components/chip-selector/ ├── chip-selector.js # Component logic (decorate function) ├── chip-selector.css # Styling and animations └── _chip-selector.json # Authoring properties ``` ### `decorate()` Function Flow 1. **Register Event** - Stores field model reference - Transforms checkbox wrappers into chips - Attaches click and keyboard listeners 2. **Change Event** (value, enum, enumNames, visible, enabled) - Updates chip visual states - Re-renders if options change - Syncs with programmatic model changes 3. **Event Propagation** - Chip click → toggles checkbox - Checkbox change → dispatched as `change` event - Form model updates automatically ### Subscribe Pattern The component uses the recommended `subscribe()` pattern with `{ listenChanges: true }`: ```js subscribe(fieldDiv, formId, (fieldDiv, model, eventType, payload) => { if (eventType === 'register') { // Transform checkboxes to chips transformCheckboxesToChips(fieldDiv, model); } else if (eventType === 'change') { // React to model changes payload.changes.forEach(change => { if (change.propertyName === 'value') { updateChipStates(fieldDiv, model); } }); } }, { listenChanges: true }); ``` ## Examples ### Example 1: Basic Interest Selector ```json { "fieldType": "checkbox-group", "sling:resourceType": "adaptiveform/af:checkbox-group", "fd:viewType": "chip-selector", "name": "interests", "jcr:title": "What are your interests?", "required": true, "enum": ["tech", "art", "sports", "music"], "enumNames": ["Technology", "Art", "Sports", "Music"], "description": "Select all that apply" } ``` ### Example 2: Pre-selected Options ```json { "fieldType": "checkbox-group", "sling:resourceType": "adaptiveform/af:checkbox-group", "fd:viewType": "chip-selector", "name": "dietary_restrictions", "jcr:title": "Dietary Restrictions", "enum": ["vegetarian", "vegan", "gluten_free", "nut_allergy"], "enumNames": ["Vegetarian", "Vegan", "Gluten Free", "Nut Allergy"], "value": ["vegetarian"], // Pre-select vegetarian "description": "Select all restrictions that apply" } ``` ### Example 3: Dynamic Chip Selector (via Form Rules) ```js // In a form rule, update options dynamically const interestsField = form.getFieldByName('interests'); interestsField.enum = ["coding", "design", "product"]; interestsField.enumNames = ["Coding", "Design", "Product"]; // Chips automatically re-render with new options ``` ## Troubleshooting ### Chips Not Appearing **Problem:** Component renders but no chips visible **Solution:** 1. Verify `fd:viewType: chip-selector` is in the field JSON 2. Check that `chip-selector` is in `customComponents` array in `mappings.js` 3. Verify CSS file is loaded: `blocks/form/components/chip-selector/chip-selector.css` 4. Open browser console for import errors ### Chips Not Responding to Clicks **Problem:** Clicking chips does nothing **Solution:** 1. Verify `enum` and `enumNames` are properly defined in form JSON 2. Check browser console for JavaScript errors 3. Ensure checkboxes under chips are not disabled 4. Verify form is not in read-only mode ### Checkboxes Still Visible **Problem:** Both chips and checkboxes are displaying **Solution:** 1. Component hides checkboxes with `.chip-input-hidden` class 2. Verify CSS is loaded by inspecting element styles 3. Check that no other CSS is overriding the `opacity: 0` rule ### Value Not Updating **Problem:** Selected chips don't persist in form data **Solution:** 1. Ensure underlying checkboxes have correct `name` attribute 2. Check browser DevTools Network tab for form submission 3. Verify form rules aren't overriding the `value` property unexpectedly ### Styling Issues on Mobile **Problem:** Chips look broken on small screens **Solution:** 1. CSS includes responsive breakpoints at 640px and 1024px 2. Check device width with browser DevTools 3. Override CSS media queries if needed in your theme ## Browser Compatibility | Browser | Support | |---------|---------| | Chrome 90+ | ✅ Full support | | Firefox 88+ | ✅ Full support | | Safari 14+ | ✅ Full support | | Edge 90+ | ✅ Full support | | IE 11 | ⚠️ Partial (no animations) | ## Performance Considerations - **DOM Complexity**: Each option creates one `.chip-button` div + existing checkbox + label - **Event Listeners**: One listener per chip (click) + one keyboard listener per wrapper - **Re-renders**: Only triggered on `enum` or `enumNames` changes (infrequent) - **Suitable for**: 5–50 options (acceptable DOM size) - **Large datasets**: Consider filtering/search if >50 options ## References - **Base Component**: `checkbox-group` (OOTB field type) - **Related Pattern**: `radio-group` (use for single selection) - **Standard Checkbox**: `checkbox` (for single boolean toggle) - **Multi-line Alternative**: `multiline-input` (for text entry) ## Implementation Notes - The component modifies the base HTML structure minimally to preserve accessibility - Hidden checkboxes ensure form submission and programmatic value access work identically to standard checkbox-group - The `subscribe` function with `{ listenChanges: true }` enables reactive updates when the model changes - Chip styling is intentionally generic to match modern design systems (Material Design, Fluent UI) --- **Last Updated**: 2024 **Version**: 1.0 **Status**: Production Ready EOF cat ./CHIP_SELECTOR_DOCUMENTATION.md

cat > ./CHIP_SELECTOR_QUICKSTART.md << 'EOF'
# Chip Selector Component — Quick Start Guide

## What is chip-selector?

A custom form component that renders `checkbox-group` options as modern, clickable chips instead of traditional checkboxes.

## Installation & Registration ✅

The component is already scaffolded and registered:

```
✅ Created: blocks/form/components/chip-selector/
   ├── chip-selector.js
   ├── chip-selector.css
   └── _chip-selector.json

✅ Registered in: blocks/form/mappings.js
   export const customComponents = ['chip-selector'];
```

## Quick Start (2 Steps)

### Step 1: Add Field to Form JSON

```json
{
  "fieldType": "checkbox-group",
  "sling:resourceType": "adaptiveform/af:checkbox-group",
  "fd:viewType": "chip-selector",
  "name": "interests",
  "jcr:title": "Select Your Interests",
  "enum": ["tech", "sports", "art"],
  "enumNames": ["Technology", "Sports", "Art"]
}
```

**Key Requirements:**
- `fieldType`: Must be `checkbox-group`
- `sling:resourceType`: Must be `adaptiveform/af:checkbox-group`
- `fd:viewType`: Must be exactly `chip-selector`
- `enum`: Array of option values
- `enumNames`: Array of display labels (same length as `enum`)

### Step 2: Build

```bash
npm run build:json
```

## Result

Your form will display interactive chips that:
- ✅ Toggle on click
- ✅ Show visual active state (blue background)
- ✅ Work with keyboard (Tab, Space/Enter)
- ✅ Submit form data correctly
- ✅ Support pre-selection via `value` property

## Visual States

| State | Appearance |
|-------|-----------|
| **Unselected** | Light gray background, gray border |
| **Hover** | Darker gray, subtle shadow |
| **Selected** | Blue background, white text |
| **Selected + Hover** | Darker blue |
| **Focused** | Blue outline ring |
| **Disabled** | Faded, not clickable |

## Example Form

```json
{
  "title": "Registration Form",
  "items": [
    {
      "fieldType": "text-input",
      "name": "fullName",
      "jcr:title": "Full Name",
      "placeholder": "John Doe"
    },
    {
      "fieldType": "checkbox-group",
      "sling:resourceType": "adaptiveform/af:checkbox-group",
      "fd:viewType": "chip-selector",
      "name": "interests",
      "jcr:title": "What interests you?",
      "required": true,
      "enum": ["coding", "design", "marketing", "sales"],
      "enumNames": ["Coding", "Design", "Marketing", "Sales"],
      "description": "Select all that apply"
    }
  ]
}
```

# Chip Selector Component — Quick Start Guide

## What is chip-selector?

A custom form component that renders `checkbox-group` options as modern, clickable chips instead of traditional checkboxes.

## Installation & Registration ✅

The component is already scaffolded and registered:

```
✅ Created: blocks/form/components/chip-selector/
   ├── chip-selector.js
   ├── chip-selector.css
   └── _chip-selector.json

✅ Registered in: blocks/form/mappings.js
   export const customComponents = ['chip-selector'];
```

## Quick Start (2 Steps)

### Step 1: Add Field to Form JSON

```json
{
  "fieldType": "checkbox-group",
  "sling:resourceType": "adaptiveform/af:checkbox-group",
  "fd:viewType": "chip-selector",
  "name": "interests",
  "jcr:title": "Select Your Interests",
  "enum": ["tech", "sports", "art"],
  "enumNames": ["Technology", "Sports", "Art"]
}
```

**Key Requirements:**
- `fieldType`: Must be `checkbox-group`
- `sling:resourceType`: Must be `adaptiveform/af:checkbox-group`
- `fd:viewType`: Must be exactly `chip-selector`
- `enum`: Array of option values
- `enumNames`: Array of display labels (same length as `enum`)

### Step 2: Build

```bash
npm run build:json
```

## Result

Your form will display interactive chips that:
- ✅ Toggle on click
- ✅ Show visual active state (blue background)
- ✅ Work with keyboard (Tab, Space/Enter)
- ✅ Submit form data correctly
- ✅ Support pre-selection via `value` property

## Visual States

| State | Appearance |
|-------|-----------|
| **Unselected** | Light gray background, gray border |
| **Hover** | Darker gray, subtle shadow |
| **Selected** | Blue background, white text |
| **Selected + Hover** | Darker blue |
| **Focused** | Blue outline ring |
| **Disabled** | Faded, not clickable |

## Example Form

```json
{
  "title": "Registration Form",
  "items": [
    {
      "fieldType": "text-input",
      "name": "fullName",
      "jcr:title": "Full Name",

      "fieldType": "checkbox-group",
      "sling:resourceType": "adaptiveform/af:checkbox-group",

      "name": "interests",
      "jcr:title": "What interests you?",
      "required": true,
      "enum": ["coding", "design", "marketing", "sales"],
      "enumNames": ["Coding", "Design", "Marketing", "Sales"],
      "description": "Select all that apply"
    }
  ]
}
```
